agent.runner_summary: The simulator creates a learner-scoped Store, compiles build_tutor_graph() with a simulator checkpoint DB, expands supported CLI shortcuts such as /lesson and /resume through the existing COMMAND_PROMPTS map, and calls german_tutor.cli.run_turn() in a worker thread while preserving one LangGraph thread_id across turns.

components.when_to_use: Use for placement regressions where the tutor chooses the right level verbally but writes the wrong CEFR value.
components.input_guidance: Use FixedComponentInput(kwargs={"level": "A1"}) with the CEFR level inferred from the transcript or reviewer feedback.
components.output_guidance: Check the returned JSON for ok=true and the normalized level value.

components.when_to_use: Use for curriculum-selection or lesson-planning regressions tied to the unit metadata the agent reads.
components.input_guidance: Use FixedComponentInput(kwargs={"unit_id": "A1.U1"}) with the exact unit id mentioned in the failing run.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned unit fields such as objectives, grammar points, and vocab theme.

components.when_to_use: Use for isolated spaced-repetition scheduling bugs without running the full tutor graph.
components.input_guidance: Use FixedComponentInput(kwargs={"ease": 2.5, "interval": 6, "reps": 2, "quality": 4}) with the original card state and review grade from logs.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned ease, interval, and reps values against the expected SM-2 transition.

log_notes: User turns appear as user_message events with the original learning-environment prompt; supported slash commands such as /lesson, /resume, /review, /placement, and /progress are expanded internally before the graph call.
Assistant turns appear as agent_message events after the selected LangGraph specialist finishes its tool loop.
LangChain tool invocations are recorded as tool_call and tool_result events extracted from AIMessage.tool_calls and ToolMessage objects in the compiled graph state.
Observed RELAI mock usage is recorded as mock_call events after each simulator turn.
learning_env_guidance: Use an end-to-end agent target for routing, off-topic refusal, lesson pacing, or any regression that depends on persistent LangGraph thread state.
Use FixedInput for command-driven flows such as /lesson, /resume, /review, /placement, or /progress when you want the simulator to exercise the same CLI shortcut expansion as the terminal app.
Use PersonaInput when the learner's persistence, confusion, or willingness to answer follow-up questions is part of the failure.

Mock store-writing or local side-effect tools such as set_level, save_lesson_pointer, cache_lesson, record_attempt, log_error, update_mastery, add_vocab, review_vocab, log_session_summary, and speak_german when you need to isolate language behavior from durable state changes or audio subprocesses.
Keep get_learner_state, get_next_unit, get_unit_details, get_cached_lesson, and get_due_vocab live when the failure depends on real curriculum or learner-state reads.
Use component targets for focused curriculum or SRS regressions before escalating to a full agent simulation.
